evidence.scientific_clarity: Scatter + OLS line + R²/slope/intercept annotation + highlighted OursModel point all present; directly fulfills the Chinchilla-style scaling law brief.
evidence.typography: Script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans — the exact default the rubric flags as bad.
evidence.palette: Blue/red/orange are distinguishable under common color-blindness types; red reserved for the fit line (not directional category), orange for emphasis — reasonable but red/green conflict avoided; palette passes basic CB check.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout() used, figsize 5.5×4.2, annotation box in upper-right, OursModel label with arrow; no visible whitespace waste or label overlaps in the PNG.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure accurately; OLS fit has a legend entry; scatter points and OursModel identified by color only (no explicit legend entries) — a reviewer may ask for a legend; caption is otherwise complete.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdf.fonttype=42 is absent from the script; pdffonts shows only Type 3 embedded fonts (DejaVu Sans variants) — text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (24 KB), figure.png (54 KB), figure.svg (49 KB), caption.tex (493 B), gen_figure.py (2.6 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption gives bold title, explains n=80 points, notes log axes, states full OLS equation with coefficients, reports R²=0.877, benchmarks against Chinchilla slope, and identifies OursModel coordinates — strong numerical claim throughout.

overall_strengths: Scientific content is complete and directly addresses the brief: scatter, OLS fit, R²/slope annotation, and highlighted OursModel point are all present and clearly readable.
Caption is detailed and quantitative, citing specific slope, intercept, R², and comparison to Chinchilla expected value — ready for a journal submission narrative.
overall_weaknesses: Vector fidelity is broken: pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, so the PDF contains only Type 3 (bitmap) fonts — text cannot be selected, searched, or re-flowed by journal production.
Typography defaults to DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default) without any override; the rubric explicitly calls this out, and no rcParams font customization is applied anywhere in the script.
